scores.notes: ba derived from Gemini bias_score 45 (inverted: 100-45=55). fc = ChatGPT factual_accuracy_score 74. af = Stage 4 completeness_score 65. ct = Stage 5 courage_score 58. sr = self-assessment after applying Stages 2-5 corrections.

headline: E-invoice exemption walked back twice in a month after Sabah loss
context: On 6 December 2025, a week after Pakatan Harapan's defeat in the Sabah state election, PM Anwar raised the e-invoice exemption from RM500,000 to RM1m turnover. On 5 January 2026 the penalty-free transition for the RM1m-5m phase was extended to end-2026. Phase 1-2 compliance was already over 90%.

cards.big: On 6 December 2025, a week after Pakatan Harapan's Sabah election loss, PM Anwar raised the e-invoice exemption to RM1m turnover.
cards.sub: SAMENTA called it relief for ~200,000 SMEs; ACCCIM put the number at 700,000-800,000. Reporting linked the timing to PH's urban vote losses in Sabah.

cards.big: Government estimates put Malaysia's shadow economy near 21% of GDP — around RM330B at 2023 values. Other methodologies range from 7% to 26.6%.
cards.sub: Khazanah's narrower 'informal sector' frame gives 4-7.8% (2019). World Economics currently reports 26.6%. The range reflects definition, not measurement.

cards.big: By 22 December 2025 LHDN had logged 820.5 million e-invoices from 111,600 taxpayers — Phase 1-2 compliance over 90%, Phase 3 above 80%.
cards.sub: Volume more than doubled from 328m in June 2025. By 27 April 2026 LHDN said e-invoice checks had surfaced RM3.5 billion in undeclared income.

cards.big: On 5 January 2026 the penalty-free transition for RM1m-5m firms was extended to end-2026. On 20 April 2026 MOF extended it again to end-2027.
cards.sub: LHDN's July 2025 e-invoice checks had already flagged 5,800 taxpayers for RM484m undeclared prior-year income, recovering about RM82m in additional tax.

cards.big: Phase 1-2 compliance: over 90%. Seven days from Sabah loss to first exemption. The system scales where enforced; the rule moves where political pressure shows.
E-invoicing works where enforced — 820m invoices, >90% Phase 1-2 compliance. Cost relief for micro firms is legitimate. But the rule walks back faster than the shadow economy shrinks.

revisions.old: (no mention of Sabah state election timing)
revisions.new: headline and hook now anchored on '6 days/a week after Pakatan Harapan's Sabah election loss'; reframe quantifies 'seven days from loss to first exemption'
revisions.primary_source: Stage 4 cited Malay Mail 6 Dec 2025 (https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2025/12/06/a-leader-that-responds-after-sabah-loss-anwar-moves-to-ease-business-burden-with-e-invoicing-threshold-adjustment-tax-refund-boost/201007). Synthesis re-verified the election occurred 29 Nov 2025 (Malay Mail, CNBC, The Diplomat, Borneo Post live blog); PH won 1 of 22 seats contested; DAP wiped out (0 of 8 previously held). Distance from polling day (29 Nov) to announcement (6 Dec) is exactly 7 days; from result announcement (30 Nov) is 6 days. 'A week after' is defensible in both framings.

revisions.old: Phase 3 (RM5-25m turnover) reached 80%. Volume more than doubled from 328m in June 2025. The CEO said the mandate is working where enforced.
revisions.new: Volume more than doubled from 328m in June 2025. By 27 April 2026 LHDN said e-invoice checks had surfaced RM3.5 billion in undeclared income.
revisions.primary_source: Stage 3 flagged the 'CEO said the mandate is working where enforced' phrasing as not a verbatim CEO quote — removed. Stage 3 omitted-fact list cited LHDN 27 Apr 2026 media statement on RM3.5B undeclared income: https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/jdnoh5we/20260427-kenyataan-media-hasil_hasil-kesan-rm35-bilion-pendapatan-tidak-lapor-berdasarkan-semakan-data-e-invois.pdf

revisions.old: One month later, on 5 January 2026, Anwar postponed e-invoicing for RM1-5m firms by another year.
revisions.new: On 5 January 2026 the penalty-free transition for RM1m-5m firms was extended to end-2026. On 20 April 2026 MOF extended it again to end-2027.
revisions.primary_source: Stage 3 flagged 'postponed... by another year' as misleading: the official action was extension of the penalty-free transition for Phase 4, not full postponement. Phase 4 began 1 Jan 2026 with consolidated e-invoice relaxation. Sources: LHDN 5 Jan 2026 media statement (https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/jgbdqgow/20260105_kenyataan-media-hasil-tempoh-peralihan-tambahan-e-invois-fasa-4.pdf); MOF 20 Apr 2026 statement on further extension to end-2027 (cited in Stage 3 omitted-facts).

revisions.old: In November 2025 LHDN alleged RM70m evasion at one luxury-car cartel. In July 2025 it flagged 5,800 individuals for RM484m undeclared income.
revisions.new: LHDN's July 2025 e-invoice checks had already flagged 5,800 taxpayers for RM484m undeclared prior-year income, recovering about RM82m in additional tax.
revisions.primary_source: Stage 3 flagged 'individuals' as imprecise (LHDN said 'taxpayers'). Stage 2 also flagged the November cartel juxtaposition as MISLEADING_FRAMING (implies SME exemption protects cartels). Both fixed by dropping the cartel mention and adding the RM82m recovery figure verified across NST 1 Aug 2025, Bernama, BusinessToday, ringgitplus, The Star 30 Jul 2025 (https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2025/07/30/lhdn-flags-5800-taxpayers-for-failure-to-submit-income-tax-forms).

revisions.old: Estimates range 21-25% across methodologies (UiTM, IJBS, World Economics). The tax gap is the explicit rationale LHDN gave for e-invoicing.
revisions.new: Khazanah's narrower 'informal sector' frame gives 4-7.8% (2019). World Economics currently reports 26.6%. The range reflects definition, not measurement.
revisions.primary_source: Stage 3 corrected World Economics figure from 25.3% → 26.6% (https://www.worldeconomics.com/Informal-Economy/Malaysia.aspx). Stage 3 also flagged the 'tax gap is the explicit rationale' as MISLEADING — MOF Fiscal Outlook 2025 says 'tax compliance, tax avoidance, and financial leakages'. Khazanah Research Institute informal-sector figure 4-7.8% added per Stage 3 omitted-facts (https://www.krinstitute.org/publications/informality-in-the-malaysian-economy).

revisions.old: (implied) e-invoicing as the sole/principal lever to narrow the shadow economy
revisions.new: RM330B framed as '2023 values'; e-invoicing framing softened across cards. Removed 'sold as the lever' rhetoric.
revisions.primary_source: Stage 3 flagged 'sold as the lever' as MISLEADING — overstates exclusivity. MOF Fiscal Outlook 2025 (https://belanjawan.mof.gov.my/pdf/belanjawan2025/revenue/fiscal_outlook_2025.pdf) describes e-invoicing as 'pivotal in reducing tax avoidance and financial leakages' (one tool, not the lever). Stage 3 noted the RM330B is a 2023 Ahmad Maslan-attributed estimate (https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2023/10/18/the-shadow-economy-is-shrinking-due-to-tin-and-e-invoice).

revisions.old: The question is not whether small firms can afford e-invoicing. It is whose books stay invisible when the rule is relaxed.
revisions.new: Phase 1-2 compliance: over 90%. Seven days from Sabah loss to first exemption. The system scales where enforced; the rule moves where political pressure shows.
revisions.primary_source: Stage 2 economic_flags: original reframe 'dismisses the legitimate economic burden of compliance on SMEs.' Stage 3 said the original reframe is rhetorical not factual. Stage 5 said the original frames 'cost relief is real / policy choice' as false symmetry, and the data shows the system works. Synthesis adopted Stage 5's harder critique: the reframe now anchors on the compliance data (Phase 1-2 >90%) and the political timing (7 days from Sabah loss), making the argument with verifiable facts instead of rhetorical assertion.

revisions.old: E-invoicing's promise was that every transaction leaves a trail. The cost relief is real. So is the policy choice to leave most of the shadow economy unrecorded.
revisions.new: E-invoicing works where enforced — 820m invoices, >90% Phase 1-2 compliance. Cost relief for micro firms is legitimate. But the rule walks back faster than the shadow economy shrinks.
revisions.primary_source: Stage 3 flagged 'every transaction' as too absolute and 'most of the shadow economy unrecorded' as OVERCLAIM (no primary source for 'most'). Stage 5 flagged the original as false symmetry. New view anchors on the verified compliance figures and explicitly acknowledges legitimate cost burden, while making the structural critique about the pattern of relaxations vs the slow-moving shadow economy.

revisions.new: Both relaxations now named (5 Jan 2026 and 20 Apr 2026); the pattern is the story
revisions.primary_source: Stage 5 cowardice_flag identified the failure to connect the back-to-back retreats to recurring political incentives. The 5 Jan and 20 Apr extensions together demonstrate the pattern.

revisions.new: ACCCIM put the number at 700,000-800,000 (alongside SAMENTA's 200,000)
revisions.primary_source: Stage 4 missing-facts list cited tax expert Datuk Kong Len Fatt of ACCCIM via enanyang.my 7 Dec 2025 (https://www.enanyang.my/news/20251207/Finance/1094871). Synthesis cross-checked — the wider 700k-800k figure reflects ACCCIM counting all micro/small firms below RM1m turnover; SAMENTA's 200k figure counts only the incremental tranche between RM500k and RM1m. Both attributable to their respective industry-body statements.

introduced_claims.claim: Sabah state election timing (29 Nov 2025) and the seven-day distance to the 6 Dec exemption announcement
introduced_claims.primary_source: Wikipedia summary verified against Malay Mail (full official results 30 Nov 2025: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2025/11/30/full-official-results-of-the-17th-sabah-election-by-party/200171); CNBC 30 Nov 2025 (https://www.cnbc.com/2025/11/30/malaysia-pm-faces-electoral-setback-as-allies-fall-in-sabah-state-polls.html); The Diplomat Dec 2025 (https://thediplomat.com/2025/12/malaysian-pm-anwar-ibrahim-suffers-setback-in-sabah-state-election/). PH won 1 of 22 seats contested; DAP wiped out (0 of 8 previously held). Polling day 29 Nov, results 30 Nov, exemption announcement 6 Dec — 7 days from polling, 6 days from result. 'A week after' covers both framings.

introduced_claims.claim: RM82m additional tax recovered from the 5,800-taxpayer / RM484m enforcement
introduced_claims.primary_source: Stage 4 omitted-facts list cited LHDN 30 Jul 2025 official statement (https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/kgvbwbo4/20250730_kenyataan-media-hasil-30-julai-2025_hasil-mengenalpasti-pembayar-cukai-tidak-patuh-berdasarkan-semakan-e-invois.pdf). Synthesis re-verified via The Star 30 Jul 2025 (https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2025/07/30/lhdn-flags-5800-taxpayers-for-failure-to-submit-income-tax-forms), NST 1 Aug 2025, Bernama, BusinessToday, ringgitplus.

introduced_claims.claim: 20 April 2026 MOF further extended Phase 4 transition to end-2027
introduced_claims.primary_source: Stage 3 omitted-facts list cited MOF 20 Apr 2026 statement (https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/ms/berita/siaran-media/perdana-menteri-umum-langkah-untuk-ringankan-tekanan-pmks-akibat-krisis-tenaga-global). Adopted into Fact 3 to reflect that the rollback is not a single event but a pattern. The MOF source URL was provided by Stage 3 — synthesis trusted the URL provenance from that stage.

introduced_claims.claim: 27 April 2026 LHDN reported RM3.5B undeclared income surfaced via e-invoice checks
introduced_claims.primary_source: Stage 3 omitted-facts list cited LHDN 27 Apr 2026 media statement (https://www.hasil.gov.my/media/jdnoh5we/20260427-kenyataan-media-hasil_hasil-kesan-rm35-bilion-pendapatan-tidak-lapor-berdasarkan-semakan-data-e-invois.pdf). Adopted into Fact 2 sub. This datapoint materially strengthens the 'system works where enforced' frame and is essential for non-misleading reporting.

synthesis_notes: Over-integration decisions: (1) Stage 2 economic_flags wanted the reframe softened to acknowledge SME cost burden; Stage 5 wanted it strengthened against false symmetry. I sided with Stage 5 on the reframe (compliance-data anchor) and with Stage 2 on the view card (explicit acknowledgment of legitimate cost burden) — splitting where the evidence supports each, not averaging across both. (2) Stage 4 wanted a gender card; Stage 5 explicitly identified gender as the weakest stress-test angle for this specific policy. Did not add a gender card — agreed with Stage 5 that the available data on differential gender impact of this specific exemption is too thin to add a verifiable card. The gender point is captured in synthesis_notes for future iterations. (3) Stage 4 wanted international comparisons (India/Indonesia/Singapore/Thailand); they were strong but would push the issue over the 1700-character bite-size budget. Held them for a possible follow-up issue. (4) Stage 4 raised East Malaysian SME infrastructure as a missing geographic angle; partially captured by anchoring the timing on Sabah, but did not add a separate East-Malaysia fact card for the same length-budget reason. (5) Stage 2 verification_log marked the late-2025/early-2026 claims as 'cannot verify independently' — this is a Gemini limitation (cutoff), not evidence of error. Stage 3 verified the same claims as VERIFIED with primary sources. Trusted Stage 3. Compression decisions: dropped the November 2025 luxury-car cartel reference entirely (Stage 2 misleading-framing flag plus length budget); dropped the original 'tax gap >RM70B/yr' figure (Stage 3 unverified-detail flag — sources cited media reports without primary methodology). Cross-issue inconsistency note: T4A issue 1701 ('Sabah State Election Called for August 2026') references an August 2026 Sabah election. The real-world Sabah election was 29 Nov 2025 (the one this issue anchors on). 1701 likely needs separate review, but resolving it is out of scope for this issue. Did not add 1701 to the related[] array.
